module.title: Wazuh SIEM Setup & Configuration
module.focus: Deploy and configure Wazuh SIEM for centralized log collection, alerting, and security monitoring
module.output: Fully operational Wazuh deployment with agents, custom rules, and alert triage capability

topics.title: Wazuh Architecture & Installation
topics.description: Deploy the complete Wazuh stack: indexer, manager, dashboard, and endpoint agents on Ubuntu
topics.content:
:::objectives
- Understand Wazuh four-component architecture
- Install Wazuh indexer, manager, and dashboard on Ubuntu
- Deploy and connect a Wazuh agent to the manager
- Verify the installation end-to-end
:::

:::info
Wazuh is an open-source security platform providing XDR and SIEM capabilities. It runs four components:

| Component | Role | Default Port |
|-----------|------|--------------|
| **Wazuh Indexer** | Stores and indexes alert data (OpenSearch fork) | 9200, 9300 |
| **Wazuh Manager** | Analyzes agent data, triggers alerts, manages policies | 1514 (agent comms), 1515 (enrollment) |
| **Wazuh Dashboard** | Web UI for visualization and management | 443 (HTTPS) |
| **Wazuh Agent** | Collects logs, syscheck, rootcheck on endpoints | N/A (pushes to manager) |
:::

### Single-Node Installation

```bash
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.7/wazuh-install.sh
sudo bash ./wazuh-install.sh -a
```

The `-a` flag installs all components on one node.

:::warning
Save the auto-generated passwords immediately. They are not stored anywhere after installation.
:::

### Agent Installation

```bash
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.7/wazuh-agent.sh
sudo bash ./wazuh-agent.sh
sudo sed -i 's/<manager-ip>/192.168.1.100/' /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf
sudo systemctl enable wazuh-agent && sudo systemctl start wazuh-agent
```

Verify on the manager:

```bash
sudo /var/ossec/bin/agent_control -l
```

:::classwork
**Lab:** Install Wazuh single-node, change admin password, install agent, confirm it appears in dashboard.
:::
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the four core Wazuh components and their roles?
topics.interviewAnswer: Indexer stores/indexes data via OpenSearch. Manager analyzes data and triggers alerts. Dashboard provides web UI. Agent collects logs and syscheck on endpoints.

quiz.question: Which command installs all Wazuh components in a single-node deployment?
options: sudo apt install wazuh-manager wazuh-indexer wazuh-dashboard
quiz.explanation: The official installer with -a flag installs indexer, manager, and dashboard on one node.

quiz.question: On which port does the Wazuh manager listen for agent connections?
quiz.explanation: Wazuh agents communicate with the manager on port 1514 (TCP).

quiz.question: After installation, what should you do with the auto-generated passwords?
options: Save them securely — they are not stored after installation
Ignore them — reset via CLI anytime
quiz.explanation: Wazuh generates unique passwords that are not saved anywhere after the script completes.

quiz.question: Which file controls Wazuh agent data collection?
quiz.explanation: The agent configuration lives at /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf.

quiz.question: What does the Wazuh syscheck module monitor?
options: File integrity — permissions, ownership, size, content changes
quiz.explanation: Syscheck is Wazuh file integrity monitoring (FIM) module.

quiz.question: Which command lists all enrolled agents on the Wazuh manager?
quiz.explanation: agent_control -l lists all agents, their IDs, names, IPs, and status.

quiz.question: What does Wazuh agent enrollment accomplish?
options: Registers agent with manager, generates ID, establishes TLS channel
quiz.explanation: Enrollment pairs agent with manager, creates unique ID, and sets up TLS on port 1514.

quiz.question: Which component stores indexed alert data using OpenSearch?
quiz.explanation: The Wazuh Indexer is the OpenSearch-based data store.

quiz.question: What alert does `sudo useradd testuser` generate in Wazuh?
options: Rule ID 5901 — new user account creation
quiz.explanation: Creating a user triggers rule 5901 (new user added to system).

quiz.question: Which port does the Wazuh API listen on by default?
quiz.explanation: The Wazuh REST API runs on port 55000 (HTTPS).

quiz.question: Which ossec.conf element defines directories to monitor with syscheck?
quiz.explanation: The <directories> element within <syscheck> specifies monitored paths.

quiz.question: What severity level is Wazuh rule 5901 (new user created)?
quiz.explanation: Rule 5901 fires at severity level 3 (Low).

quiz.question: What happens when the Wazuh agent cannot reach the manager?
options: Queues events locally and retries every 10 seconds
quiz.explanation: Wazuh agents buffer events locally and retry connection every 10 seconds by default.

quiz.question: Which flag in the installer performs all-in-one installation?
quiz.explanation: The -a flag installs indexer, manager, and dashboard on a single node.

quiz.question: What is the correct startup order of Wazuh components?
options: Indexer first, then Manager, then Dashboard
Dashboard first, then Manager, then Indexer
Manager first, then Dashboard, then Indexer
quiz.explanation: Indexer must start first as data store, then Manager, then Dashboard.

topics.title: Wazuh Agent Configuration & Alert Tuning
topics.description: Configure agent data collection, assign groups, tune rules, reduce false positives
topics.content:
:::objectives
- Edit agent ossec.conf for specific monitoring modules
- Assign agents to groups for centralized policy management
- Create custom decoders and rules
- Understand alert severity levels and filtering
:::

### Agent Groups

```bash
sudo /var/ossec/bin/agent_groups -a -g web_servers -q
sudo /var/ossec/bin/agent_groups -a -i 001 -g web_servers -q
```

### Custom Decoders

```xml
<decoder name="sshd-failed-auth">
  <pre_match>Failed password for invalid user </pre_match>
  <regex>from (\S+) port \d+</regex>
  <order>srcip</order>
</decoder>
```

### Custom Rules

```xml
<rule id="100100" level="10">
  <if_sid>5712</if_sid>
  <field name="srcip">!192.168.1.0/24</field>
  <description>SSH brute force from external IP</description>
</rule>
```

### Alert Severity Levels

| Level | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| 0 | Ignore |
| 1-3 | Low |
| 4-7 | Medium |
| 8-11 | High |
| 12-15 | Critical |
| 16 | Emergency |

:::warning
Always test custom rules with `wazuh-logtest` before deploying to production.
:::

:::classwork
**Lab:** Enable auth.log monitoring, create a custom rule for SSH brute force, test with wazuh-logtest.
:::
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you reduce false positives in Wazuh?
topics.interviewAnswer: Use IP whitelisting, increase thresholds, narrow log sources, and validate rules with wazuh-logtest before deployment.

quiz.question: Which ossec.conf section configures FIM?
quiz.explanation: The <syscheck> section defines FIM parameters.

quiz.question: What does the `frequency` tag in `<syscheck>` control?
quiz.explanation: Frequency is scan interval in seconds (43200 = 12 hours default).

quiz.question: What severity is a "Critical" Wazuh alert?
quiz.explanation: Levels 12-15 are Critical severity.

quiz.question: Which tool tests log lines against decoders and rules?
quiz.explanation: wazuh-logtest shows which decoder and rule match a log line.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of Wazuh agent groups?
options: Apply shared config to multiple agents
quiz.explanation: Groups allow centralized policy management across agents.

quiz.question: How do you assign agent 001 to "web_servers" group?
quiz.explanation: agent_groups with -a (add), -i (ID), -g (group), -q (quiet) assigns agents.

quiz.question: Which Ubuntu log file contains SSH auth events?
quiz.explanation: Ubuntu uses /var/log/auth.log; RHEL uses /var/log/secure.

quiz.question: What does `<if_sid>5712</if_sid>` mean in a rule?
options: Rule only fires if 5712 already matched
quiz.explanation: <if_sid> makes a rule conditional on the parent rule matching first.

quiz.question: What does `realtime="yes"` in syscheck do?
options: Uses inotify for real-time file monitoring
Forces immediate scan on agent start
quiz.explanation: realtime="yes" uses Linux inotify for immediate file change detection.

quiz.question: Which field shows source IP in an SSH brute force alert?
quiz.explanation: The sshd decoder extracts source IP into data.srcip.

quiz.question: What is the default syscheck scan frequency?
quiz.explanation: Default frequency is 43200 seconds (12 hours).

quiz.question: What does a Wazuh rule with level 0 do?
quiz.explanation: Level 0 means no alert is generated.

quiz.question: Which ossec.conf element whitelists IPs?
quiz.explanation: <white_list> excludes IPs from generating alerts.

quiz.question: What does "Phase 3: Completed filtering (rules)" mean in wazuh-logtest?
options: Decoder matched and rules were evaluated
quiz.explanation: Phase 3 is rule matching after decoding.

quiz.question: Where are group configs stored on the Wazuh manager?
quiz.explanation: Group configs are in /var/etc/shared/<group_name>/.
